meaning: A good thing that seemed bad at first
example: Losing that job was a blessing in disguise because it led me to a better career.

meaning: To decide to do something difficult or unpleasant that one has been putting off
example: I hate going to the dentist, but I just have to bite the bullet and go.

meaning: To stop working on something
example: We have been working on this project for hours, let's call it a day.

meaning: Feeling ill or sick
example: I'm going to stay in bed today because I am feeling a bit under the weather.

meaning: Something that is very easy to do
example: The math test was a piece of cake; I finished it in ten minutes.

meaning: A way to wish someone good luck, especially before a performance
example: You will do great in the school play tonight. Break a leg!

word: cost an arm and a leg
meaning: To be very expensive
example: I would love to buy that new bicycle, but it costs an arm and a leg.

word: let the cat out of the bag
meaning: To share information that was intended to be a secret
example: We were planning a surprise party, but my brother let the cat out of the bag.

word: hit the nail on the head
meaning: To describe exactly what is causing a situation or problem
example: You hit the nail on the head when you said we need more time to study.

meaning: Happening very rarely
example: My sister lives in another country, so I only see her once in a blue moon.

meaning: Said when a person appears just after being mentioned
example: We were just talking about Mary, and speak of the devil, here she comes!

meaning: To agree with someone
example: My brother and I don't always see eye to eye on which movie to watch.

meaning: Something that will never happen
example: I will clean my room every single day when pigs fly!

word: kill two birds with one stone
meaning: To solve two problems at once
example: I read my English book on the train to work, killing two birds with one stone.

meaning: An ideal situation where you can enjoy two different things at the same time
example: Working from home allows me to have the best of both worlds: earning money and spending time with my family.

meaning: To do something badly or cheaply to save time or money
example: If you cut corners when building a house, it might not be safe.

meaning: To make a bad situation even worse
example: First my car broke down, and to add insult to injury, it started pouring rain.

word: judge a book by its cover
meaning: To make a decision based only on appearances
example: The restaurant looked tiny and old, but the food was amazing. You really can't judge a book by its cover.

meaning: To go to sleep
example: It has been a long day, and I am ready to hit the sack.

meaning: To miss an opportunity
example: I waited too long to buy the tickets, and now they are sold out. I really missed the boat.

meaning: What you do is more important than what you say
example: He promised to help, but actions speak louder than words, and he never showed up.

meaning: Looking in the wrong place or accusing the wrong person
example: If you think I broke your phone, you're barking up the wrong tree.

meaning: To avoid talking about what is important
example: Stop beating around the bush and tell me what happened!

word: bite off more than you can chew
meaning: To take on a task that is way too big
example: I think I bit off more than I could chew by joining three different sports teams.

meaning: To destroy a relationship in a way that it cannot be fixed
example: You shouldn't burn bridges with your old friends even if you move away.

word: by the skin of your teeth
meaning: To just barely succeed or escape
example: I passed the difficult math test by the skin of my teeth.

meaning: To catch someone doing something wrong
example: The teacher caught him red-handed passing notes in class.

meaning: To ignore someone on purpose
example: I tried to say hello, but she gave me the cold shoulder.

meaning: A lazy person who watches too much television
example: Don't be a couch potato; let's go play outside!

meaning: To be upset over something that has already happened and cannot be changed
example: You lost the game, but there is no use crying over spilled milk. Try again next time.

meaning: Being too curious can get you into trouble
example: I wouldn't open that box if I were you. Curiosity killed the cat, you know.

word: don't put all your eggs in one basket
meaning: Don't risk everything on one single plan or idea
example: Apply to several schools; don't put all your eggs in one basket.

meaning: Realistic, practical, and friendly
example: Even though she is famous, she is very down to earth.

meaning: A very small or unimportant amount
example: The money we raised is just a drop in the bucket compared to what we need.

meaning: The person who arrives first has the best chance for success
example: We should get to the amusement park right when it opens. The early bird catches the worm!

meaning: An obvious problem that no one wants to talk about
example: His bad grades were the elephant in the room during dinner.

word: every cloud has a silver lining
meaning: There is a positive side to every bad situation
example: I failed the test, but every cloud has a silver lining: now I know what to study.

meaning: To accept the unpleasant results of your actions
example: I broke the window, and now I have to face the music.

meaning: To become too nervous to do something
example: He was supposed to sing on stage, but he got cold feet at the last minute.

meaning: To become difficult to control
example: The party started getting out of hand when people started throwing food.

meaning: To help someone
example: Can you give me a hand with these heavy bags?

meaning: To do more than what is expected

meaning: Very well behaved

meaning: Don't give up

meaning: Not paying attention to what is happening around you

meaning: Wait a moment; be patient

meaning: In the same difficult situation

meaning: To watch someone or something carefully
example: Please keep an eye on my bag while I go to the restroom.

meaning: Do not interfere with a situation because it could cause trouble

word: like two peas in a pod
meaning: Very similar, especially in appearance or personality
example: The twins are like two peas in a pod; they do everything together.

meaning: To quickly tell the main points of a story
example: To make a long story short, we got lost and missed the movie.

meaning: You have to work hard to achieve something
example: My legs hurt from running, but no pain, no gain!

meaning: Extremely happy
example: She was on cloud nine when she won the spelling bee.

meaning: In a risky or dangerous situation
example: You are on thin ice if you keep coming to class late.

meaning: Suddenly and unexpectedly

meaning: To decide how to deal with a situation as it develops
example: We don't know what time we will arrive, so let's just play it by ear.

meaning: To joke with someone by telling them something untrue

meaning: To spoil someone's plans or happiness

meaning: Raining very heavily

meaning: A general rule or guideline
example: As a rule of thumb, you should drink 8 glasses of water a day.

meaning: To save money for a time when it might be needed
example: I got some money for my birthday, but I'm saving it for a rainy day.

meaning: Very sick
example: I couldn't go to school yesterday because I was sick as a dog.

meaning: To reveal a secret
example: Come on, spill the beans! What did you get Mom for her birthday?

meaning: To take the attention away from someone else
example: My sister stole my thunder by announcing her engagement at my graduation party.

meaning: To decline an invitation but offer to do it at a later time
example: I'm busy tonight, but can I take a rain check on that movie?

word: take it with a grain of salt
meaning: To not completely believe something that you are told
example: You should take his stories with a grain of salt; he loves to exaggerate.

word: the ball is in your court
meaning: It is your turn to make a decision or take action
example: I have made my offer, so now the ball is in your court.

word: the best thing since sliced bread
meaning: An excellent new invention or idea
example: My new smartphone is the best thing since sliced bread.

meaning: Only a small part of a much larger problem

meaning: To give up

meaning: Time passes very quickly

meaning: Feeling slightly ill

meaning: Uncertain or undecided
example: Our holiday plans are still up in the air.

meaning: Something that happened in the past and is no longer important
example: We used to argue a lot, but that is all water under the bridge now.

meaning: I completely agree with you
example: 'It is extremely hot today!' 'You can say that again!'

word: your guess is as good as mine
meaning: I have no idea
example: 'What time will the bus arrive?' 'Your guess is as good as mine.'

meaning: A way of asking someone what they are thinking about
example: You have been quiet for a while. A penny for your thoughts?

meaning: What someone does is more important than what they say
example: He said he would help, but actions speak louder than words.

word: at the drop of a hat

meaning: To start over because a previous attempt failed

meaning: People who make big threats rarely carry them out

meaning: It is better to do something late than not do it at all

word: birds of a feather flock together
meaning: People with similar interests or characters usually spend time together

meaning: To start a conversation and make people feel more comfortable

meaning: To stop what you are doing for the rest of the night and go to bed

meaning: Not clear at all; confusing

meaning: No matter what happens

meaning: Very calm and relaxed

word: cross that bridge when you come to it

word: don't count your chickens before they hatch
meaning: Don't rely on something happening before it actually happens

meaning: There's no point in being upset over something that has already happened and cannot be changed

meaning: Very easy

meaning: Everyone will have good luck or success at some point in their lives

meaning: Alert and fully aware of what is happening

meaning: Something very easy to do

meaning: Doing something repeatedly is the only way to become very good at it

meaning: To try your very best

meaning: To look for or discover a hidden meaning

meaning: From the original or most reliable source

meaning: Someone who is cherished above all others

meaning: To give up or accept defeat
